title: How Kenyan Creators Get Paid Internationally: My Unfiltered Take After Helping Hundreds of Creators
author: Unchained Commerce
excerpt: Selling digital products from Nigeria, Ghana, Kenya, Pakistan, or Bangladesh is possible with crypto-native infrastructure that has no geographic restrictions.

content.text: As a Ghanaian digital creator, I know firsthand the frustration of trying to get paid internationally without access to PayPal or Stripe. Every delayed payment, every lost sale, and every missed opportunity to scale my business is a nail in the coffin of my dreams. I've seen countless creators stuck in limbo, unable to tap into the global market due to restrictive payment systems. It's time to break free from these chains and discover the truth about getting paid internationally as a Kenyan creator – or rather, as any creator from a restricted country. In this article, I'll share my unfiltered take after helping hundreds of creators just like you.
The Honest Reality Most Creators Avoid

The harsh reality is that most payment systems, including PayPal and Stripe, come with strict geographic and banking restrictions. This means that creators from countries like Kenya, Nigeria, and Ghana are often left out in the cold, unable to access the same financial opportunities as their Western counterparts. These restrictions are designed to mitigate risk, but they ultimately hurt the creators who need these tools the most. By ignoring these limitations, I've seen creators lose thousands of dollars in potential revenue, forcing them to abandon their passions and side hustles.
Why the Popular Advice Is Wrong

You've probably heard that you need to 'just use another payment processor' or 'find a local bank that works with international transactions.' While these suggestions might seem like a viable solution, they often lead to more headaches and financial losses. Most alternative payment processors come with their own set of restrictions, fees, or complicated KYC requirements. These workarounds rarely provide the seamless, hassle-free experience that creators need to thrive in the global market.
What the Data and Experience Actually Show

After years of working with creators from restricted countries, I've seen a clear pattern emerge: the best solution to international payment issues lies in blockchain-based payment systems. These decentralized platforms offer a more open, transparent, and secure way to facilitate transactions, free from the constraints of traditional payment processors. With the right tools, creators can access a global customer base, automate their sales processes, and focus on what matters most – creating amazing content and building their brand.
Don't wait for the perfect payment solution – take action and start exploring blockchain-based options today.
The Uncomfortable Truth About Your Options

Let's face it: there are no perfect solutions when it comes to getting paid internationally. Every payment system comes with its own set of trade-offs, and there's no one-size-fits-all answer. However, the data and my experience show that blockchain-based payment systems offer the most promise for creators from restricted countries. By embracing this technology and leveraging the right tools, you can overcome the obstacles that have held you back for so long.
What You Should Do Based on All of This

So, what's the takeaway from all this? If you're a Kenyan creator looking to get paid internationally, it's time to explore blockchain-based payment systems. With the right tools and a willingness to adapt, you can break free from the chains of restricted payment systems and unlock a world of possibilities. Start by assessing your current situation, identifying the pain points that hold you back, and researching the various payment solutions available to you. From there, take the first step towards a more secure, transparent, and global payment experience.
